content.problemStatement:
Given a sorted array of integers and a value xxx, count the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that i<ji < ji<j and the sum of arr[i]arr[i]arr[i] +++ arr[j]arr[j]arr[j] is less than xxx.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer nnn, the number of elements in the array.
The second line contains nnn integers representing the elements of the sorted array.
The third line contains the integer xxx.


Output Format
Output a single integer, the number of pairs whose sum is less than xxx.
constraints: The array a is sorted in non‑decreasing order.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the size of the sorted array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers in non‑decreasing order. Third line contains the integer X.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of pairs (i, j) with i < j and a[i] + a[j] < X.

solution.approach: Because the array is sorted we can use the two‑pointer technique. Keep a left pointer l at the start and a right pointer r at the end. If a[l] + a[r] < X, then for this l all elements between l+1 and r form valid pairs with a[l]; we add (r‑l) to the answer and move l forward. Otherwise the sum is too large, so we move r backward. This scans the array once, giving O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (besides the input array).
